zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Colville於1379年出生在英國越可爾阿可利福時，她的父親John Colville32歲，母親Alice Darcy26歲。她於大約1397年與William Mauleverer III在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1463年在英國越可爾阿勒頓享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾阿勒頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Colville于1379年出生在英国越可爾阿可利福时，她的父亲John Colville32岁，母亲Alice Darcy26岁。她于大约1397年与William Mauleverer III在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1463年在英国越可爾阿勒頓享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾阿勒頓。

nameMeanings.name: Colville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Colleville in Seine-Maritime, France, named with the Scandinavian personal name Koli (see Cole 2) + Old French ville ‘settlement, village’. The Scottish branch of the family were descended from Philip de Colville, who came to Scotland as a household knight of kings Malcolm IV (1153–65) and William ‘the Lion’ (1165–1214).

variants.name: Cole
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colvin
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal, Antrim): variant of   Colville  , probably reflecting a local pronunciation. The name was taken to Ulster in the 17th century.
Manx: if not identical with 1, perhaps from the Old Norse personal name Kolbeinn, with /v/ substituted for /b/.

English: from the rare Middle English personal name Colwin, Colvin, which may be a borrowing into English of a Welsh name whose modern form is Collwyn ‘white’, or of colwyn ‘doe, puppy, pet dog’, or of the placename Colwyn (Denbighshire, Radnorshire).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Colville was born in 1379, in Arncliffe, Yorkshire, England, her father, John Colville, was 32 and her mother, Alice Darcy, was 26. She married William Mauleverer III about 1397, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1463, in Allerton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Allerton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Colville est née en 1379, à Arncliffe, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Colville, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Alice Darcy, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé William Mauleverer III environ 1397, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1463, à Allerton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Allerton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Colville 1379 in Arncliffe, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Colville 32 und ihre Mutter Alice Darcy 26. Sie hat um 1397 in Yorkshire, England William Mauleverer III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1463 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Allerton, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Allerton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Colville è nata il nel 1379 nel Arncliffe, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John Colville, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Alice Darcy, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato William Mauleverer III circa 1397, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1463, nel Allerton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolta a Allerton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Colvilleが1379年にイギリスヨークシュアイアアルンクリフで生まれた時，父John Colvilleは32歳，母Alice Darcyは26歳であった。約1397年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでWilliam Mauleverer IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1463年にイギリスヨークシュアイアアラートンで84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアアラートンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Colville(이)가 1379년 영국 요르크실르 아클리프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Colville(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Darcy(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1397년 영국 요르크실르에서 William Mauleverer III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1463년 영국 요르크실르 앨레르턴에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 앨레르턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Colville nasceu em 1379, em Arncliffe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Colville, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Alice Darcy, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com William Mauleverer III aproximadamente 1397, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1463, em Allerton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Allerton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Colville родилась в 1379 г. в местности арнклифф, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Colville было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Darcy, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Mauleverer III около 1397 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1463 г. в местности аллиртон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности аллиртон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Colville nació en 1379, en Arncliffe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Colville, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Alice Darcy, tenía 26 años. Se casó con William Mauleverer III aproximadamente 1397, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1463, en Allerton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en Allerton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
